Calanthe is a genus of flowering plants in the orchid family, characterized by their showy, fragrant flowers and unique growth habits. Some species of Calanthe, including those in the Calanthe genus, have been studied for their potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Calanthe species include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to their medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Calanthe species include teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to support immune function and alleviate respiratory issues.
